steps.title: Group like terms
steps.content: Add the two polynomials by combining matching terms: $$(3x^2+2x-8)+(x^2+5x+2)$$ $$=(3x^2+x^2)+(2x+5x)+(-8+2)$$

steps.title: Simplify each group
steps.content: The $x^2$ terms give $4x^2$, the $x$ terms give $7x$, and the constants give $-6$. So the expression is $$4x^2+7x-6$$ Choices A and B miss one $x^2$ term, while choice C has the wrong constant sign. The answer is D.
